revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:浅草富士浅間神社]]
{{Infobox religious building |名称 = 浅草富士浅間神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = File:Asakusa Fuji Sengen jinja.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:東京都|東京都]][[:en:台東区|台東区]][[:en:浅草|浅草]]5-3-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|43|9.4|N|139|47|53.9|E|display=inline,title|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity = [[Konohanasakuya-hime|木花咲耶比売命]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = お富士さん |札所等 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = 7月1日 |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Taitō-ku|qid=Q232641|1=Taitō}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.719277777777776|N|139.79830555555554|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Asakusa Fuji Sengen Shrine''' (浅草富士浅間神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Asakusa|en|Asakusa|qid=Q720644|lt=Asakusa|1=Asakusa}}, {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|qid=Q232641|lt=Taitō, Tokyo|1=Taitō}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. It is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Asakusa Shrine|en|Asakusa Shrine|qid=Q670049|lt=Asakusa Shrine|1=Asakusa Shrine}}.<ref name="official">[https://www.asakusajinja.jp/sengenjinja/ Asakusa Fuji Sengen Shrine] - Asakusa Shrine Official Website</ref> == Overview == The shrine is situated north of the pathway between {{ill|Sensō-ji|en|Sensō-ji|qid=Q615183|lt=Sensō-ji|1=Sensō-ji}}'s main hall and Asakusa Shrine, along the road known as Fuji Street. It stands across from {{ill|Taitō City Fuji Elementary School|ja|台東区立富士小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taitō City Fuji Elementary School|1=Fuji Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11411953}} and diagonally opposite the {{ill|Asakusa Police Station|ja|浅草警察署|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakusa Police Station|1=Asakusa Police Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557240}}. The exact date of the shrine’s establishment is unknown, but it is one of the many {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|qid=Q3471785|lt=Sengen shrines|1=Sengen shrine}} built across Japan following the rise of {{ill|Fujiko (religion)|en|Fujiko (religion)|qid=Q11456201|lt=Fuji faith|1=Fujiko (religion)}} during the Edo period. Initially located in Miyoshi-chō (present-day {{ill|Kuramae, Tokyo|ja|蔵前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramae, Tokyo|1=Kuramae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11621884}}), it was relocated to its current site by order of the Tokugawa shogunate. Based on ridge plaques found in the main hall, this relocation likely occurred during the {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|qid=Q1325001|lt=Genroku era|1=Genroku}} (1688–1703).<ref name="official"/> During the Edo period, the shrine was managed by {{ill|Shūzen-ji (Asakusa)|ja|修善寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūzen-ji (Asakusa)|1=Shūzen-ji (Asakusa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11386304}}, a sub-temple of Sensō-ji, and was worshiped as a syncretic Fuji Gongen shrine under {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|qid=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} practices. However, following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} policy of the Meiji era, it became an auxiliary shrine of Asakusa Shrine in {{ill|1873|en|1873|qid=Q7757|lt=Meiji 6|1=1873}} (1873).<ref name="official"/> == Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Fujizuka|en|Fujizuka|qid=Q5507555|lt=Fujizuka|1=Fujizuka}} (Asakusa Fuji) * Shrine office and rental conference hall == Events == * **Plant Market (植木市)** – A traditional event dating back to the Edo period. Held over four days on the last Saturday and Sunday of May and June. During this time, {{ill|Yanagi-dori|ja|浅草柳通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagi-dori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, two streets west of Fuji Street, is turned into a pedestrian zone with numerous bonsai and potted plant vendors. Additionally, street stalls line {{ill|Ichiyōzakura Komatsubashi-dori|ja|一葉桜・小松橋通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiyōzakura Komatsubashi-dori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which runs in front of the shrine.<ref name="aks">[https://asakusa-kannonura.jp/uekiichi.html Otomisan’s Plant Market] - Asakusa Kannon-ura Promotion Association</ref><ref>[https://www.city.taito.lg.jp/event/kanko/ohujisannouekiichi.html Otomisan’s Plant Market] - Taitō City</ref> ** The distribution of {{ill|Mugiwara Ja|ja|麦藁蛇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugiwara Ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (straw snake amulets) also takes place during this event.<ref name="official"/><ref name="aks"/> == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.asakusajinja.jp/sengenjinja/ Asakusa Fuji Sengen Shrine] - Asakusa Shrine Official Website {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|qid=Q3471785|lt=Sengen shrines|1=Sengen shrine}} {{Shinto-stub|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|浅草富士浅間神社|version=101967625|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:浅草富士浅間神社]]
